title: crepis bellidifolia
intro: Crepis bellidifolia, also known as the dandelion hawk's beard or false dandelion, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and leafy greens. Traditionally, Crepis bellidifolia has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's health benefits include its potential to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Crepis bellidifolia can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
